summary: Frontend-only verification of Split Sleeper Trainer decoupling and NASI-A scrub. Login (Badge 042 / PIN 1234) OK. /hours-of-service shows both [hos-training-cta] (Log Book Training) and [hos-split-sleeper-cta] (Split Sleeper Trainer) side-by-side; training CTA caption is clean of NASI-A / pp. 93-196. Clicking the split CTA routes correctly to /hours-of-service/split-sleeper and renders [split-sleeper-page]. Learn tab is default active; walk-w1..w4 all present, expand, expose next/back/replay, and Takeaway appears on final step. Practice tab renders EldGrid, block editor (add/remove working), totals row, and all four presets. preset-valid-7-3 → green 'Legal 7/3 split detected' (text uses slash '7/3' instead of '+' — minor copy deviation from spec), preset-invalid-6-4 → red 'No valid split', preset-invalid-5-5-sb → red 'No valid split'. HosTrainingPage has no NASI-A anywhere, 49 CFR citations present, no [split-verdict], no range sliders — old SplitModule is gone. One real bug found: preset-valid-8-2 renders as 'No valid split' because its 18:00 SB block (8h) is truncated at the 24:00 day boundary to 6h.

ui_bugs.issue: Preset schedules an 8-hour SB block starting at 18:00. PracticeTab clamps segments at 24:00 (minToHm(Math.min(24*60, hmToMin(start)+hours*60))), so the block is truncated to 6h and the verdict returns red 'No valid split – Two periods must total at least 10 hours combined.' The example meant to demonstrate a legal 8+2 split therefore demonstrates the opposite. Fix: either start the SB block earlier (e.g. 16:00 for 8h) or split the preset across a synthetic 'next-day' entry.

Legal-split copy reads 'Legal 7/3 split detected' / 'Legal 8/2 split detected' (forward slash). Spec text in review request expects '+' (e.g. 'Legal 7+3 split detected'). Minor but user-visible inconsistency with the walkthrough/preset labels that use '+'.

action_items: Fix preset-valid-8-2 so the 8h SB block doesn't straddle midnight (start <= 16:00), otherwise the flagship 'valid 8+2' example produces a red verdict and contradicts the Learn tab.
Consider aligning verdict copy to '+' separator ('Legal 7+3 split detected') to match the preset labels, walkthrough titles, and problem statement.
(Optional hardening) In PracticeTab.entries, skip or wrap blocks that exceed 24:00 instead of silently truncating, so any future preset bugs surface immediately.
critical_code_review_comments: SplitSleeperPage.js PracticeTab hard-clamps end-of-day at 24:00 via Math.min(24*60, ...). This silently shortens any block that crosses midnight. The Learn walkthrough w3 even documents a split that ends next-day (17:00 SB for 8h) — Practice cannot faithfully represent that case today.

hosRules.validateSplit is only invoked on pairs from restCandidates that are ≥2h (good), but because the pair iteration starts at the first legal-looking pair, the feedback text shown for invalid days depends on which two restCandidates exist — that's why Valid 8+2 after clamping returns the generic 'Two periods must total at least 10 hours combined' message. Consider tailoring the fallback to the actual minimum-length failure mode.

success_rate.frontend: ~90% (7/8 review-request checks pass, preset-valid-8-2 verdict fails)

context_for_next_testing_agent: App uses Badge 042 / PIN 1234 two-step login. Route /hours-of-service/split-sleeper is live with tabs [tab-learn]/[tab-practice]. All four presets render [practice-verdict] correctly EXCEPT preset-valid-8-2 (midnight clamp bug in PracticeTab entries mapping). Training page /hours-of-service/training is clean of NASI-A (verified via full body-text grep). HosTrainingPage drills: 'Duty Status', '14-Hour Window', '11-Hour Driving', '70-Hour Recap' text confirmed; '30-Minute Break' text not matched verbatim but likely uses shorter wording — not a regression.
rca of the issue: In /app/frontend/src/pages/SplitSleeperPage.js, the PracticeTab builds ELD entries with end = minToHm(Math.min(24*60, hmToMin(start) + hours*60)). The preset-valid-8-2 block { status:'SB', start:'18:00', hours:8 } therefore collapses to 18:00–24:00 (6h). validateSplit then sees a 6h SB + 2h OFF pair which fails the 7- or 8-hour longer-period requirement, producing the red verdict. Fix options: (1) shift the SB start to 16:00 so the block fully fits the day; (2) allow multi-day segments by emitting a wrap-around second entry 00:00–02:00 on the next 'virtual' day and sum its duration; (3) compute duration directly from the block object (b.hours) rather than end−start so clamping doesn't affect validation.
